description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the python:3.5 Docker image globally and node:12 for JavaScript jobs, with a redis:latest and postgres:9.5 service for the Django tests. Define three stages: test, package, and unit_tests, and set a cache keyed by $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_ID that stores ${PIP_CACHE}, ${APP}/frontend/node_modules, and ${VENV_DIR}. In the package stage add a buildwheels job that runs apt‑get update, installs apt‑transport‑https, wget, adds the NodeSource repo, installs nodejs and libldap2-dev/libsasl2-dev, then pip3 ${PIP_TRUSTED_HOSTS} --cache-dir=${PIP_CACHE} install -r requirements/build.txt and pip3 wheel --wheel-dir=${WHEELHOUSE} ., publishing the ${WHEELHOUSE} directory as a "packaged-wheels" artifact that expires in one hour. In the test stage add a djangotests job (with the global before_script creating ${PIP_CACHE}, a virtualenv at ${VENV_DIR} and activating it) that installs libldap2-dev/libsasl2-dev, copies configuration, generates RSA keys and a self‑signed certificate with openssl, installs dev requirements via pip3, runs python setup.py develop, and finally executes ${APP} test; also add javascript_lint (node:12, before_script cd ${APP}/frontend && npm install, script npm run lint), javascript_test (node:12, before_script sets timezone, cd ${APP}/frontend && npm install, script npm test), python_lint (allow_failure true, script pip3 ${PIP_TRUSTED_HOSTS} --cache-dir=${PIP_CACHE} install flake8 && flake8), and security_checks (script pip3 ${PIP_TRUSTED_HOSTS} --cache-dir=${PIP_CACHE} install bandit==1.6.2 && bandit -r ${APP} -x ${APP}/*/migrations,${APP}/frontend).
